name: Tearable DOM
blurb: Click and drag to rip the page in half and reveal the raw HTML underneath.

name: Glass Shatter
blurb: Click a link and the page shatters into hundreds of glass shards that fly at you, then reassemble.

name: The Looming Shadow
blurb: A massive shadow sweeps across, inverting colors and revealing hidden text where it touches.

name: Burning Paper
blurb: Scroll too fast and digital friction ignites the page, burning text into drifting ash.

name: Magnifying Burn
blurb: Hover too long and a magnifying glass scorches a black hole through the page.

name: Inflatable Divs
blurb: Click elements to pump them full of air until they bulge, distort, and POP.

name: Sand Clock DOM
blurb: Text turns to sand and falls to the floor. Read it before it dissolves.

name: The Zipper
blurb: The page is sealed shut. Drag a giant 3D zipper down to open the content.

name: The Breathing Site
blurb: Every margin and padding inhales and exhales — the whole layout breathes like lungs.

name: The De-constructor
blurb: A button un-writes the HTML, tag by tag, into a blank white void.

name: Marquee Singularity
blurb: It's 1998 and every element is a <marquee>, scrolling in all directions at once.

name: The Glitch Dimension
blurb: Find the hidden pixel to corrupt the DOM into glitch-art horror with a live WebGL shader.

name: 4D Hypercube UI
blurb: Navigation is a projected 4D tesseract — rotate through the fourth dimension to reach links.

name: Code City 3D
blurb: The source maps into a 3D metropolis — functions are towers, variables are cars.

name: Internet Traffic Simulator
blurb: A WebGL globe of live attacks; your cursor is the defense shield.

name: The Final Boss Website
blurb: Fight the developer's avatar in a bullet-hell to download the résumé.

name: Generative Conspiracy Map
blurb: Name any two things; a 3D node graph 'proves' how they're secretly connected.

name: Browser Fingerprint Art
blurb: A 3D sculpture generated from your exact browser fingerprint — unique to your machine.

name: Dream Interpreter DOM
blurb: Type a dream; a WebGL surrealist sky is painted from it and locked as the background.

name: The Traceroute Highway
blurb: A 3D racer whose track is built from the server hops your packet took to get here.

name: Div-Eating Snake
blurb: Play Snake over the article — eat the paragraphs to reveal hidden images beneath.

name: Breakout DOM
blurb: Classic Breakout, but the bricks are this site's real content containers.

name: Z-Index Ocean
blurb: Ten thousand stacked layers hide the content. Dig down through the z-index to find it.

name: DOM Jenga
blurb: A tower of CSS blocks. Pull them out — but collapse the layout and your session ends.

name: Scrollbar Labyrinth
blurb: No mouse navigation — solve a maze purely by balancing the two scrollbars.

name: The Physics Volume Slider
blurb: A volume knob on a pendulum. Throw it and hit Save the instant it swings past your target.

name: Terms of Service Speedrun
blurb: The Accept button falls down a bottomless pit of legalese. Race it before it vanishes.

name: The Sniper UI
blurb: The Checkout button is tiny and teleports twice a second. Snipe it through a scope.

name: The Boids Navigation
blurb: The menu links flock like birds. Chase one down and catch it to navigate.

name: Pathfinding Unsubscribe
blurb: The Unsubscribe button uses pathfinding to flee your cursor and hide behind elements.

name: Magnifying Glass UI
blurb: The whole article is rendered at 1px. Your cursor is a giant loupe — move it to read.

name: The HTTP Status Dungeon
blurb: A dungeon you navigate by editing the URL's status code — 403 locks doors, 418 heals.

name: Console.log() Adventure
blurb: A text RPG with a blank screen — played entirely by typing functions into the DevTools console.

name: The Honeypot Simulator
blurb: Visualize live brute-force bot attacks as a tower-defense game and stop the breach.

name: The RSA Puzzle Box
blurb: Crack a deliberately tiny RSA key by hand — factor it, find d, decrypt the secret.

name: The Mirror Site
blurb: The copy rewrites itself to perfectly match the tone of wherever you came from.

name: Auto-Translating Chaos
blurb: Routed through translation 50 times — poetic, broken, surreal English. Slide to degrade.

name: Nostalgia Engine
blurb: The CSS rewrites itself to look exactly like the web of the year you were born.

name: Reverse Search
blurb: The site searches YOU — weirder and weirder yes/no questions until it guesses what you want.

name: The Deceptive Scrollbar
blurb: Reaching the bottom expands the page forever with endless generated filler.

name: Read-Mind Predictor
blurb: A model predicts your next click from cursor velocity and pre-highlights the button.

name: Time-Travel History
blurb: A slider that morphs the UI backward and forward through decades of web design.

name: The API Blackhole
blurb: Random data from many feeds, woven by an engine into one cohesive surreal story.

name: The 1ms Latency Challenge
blurb: Watch a packet race 100 servers around a world map to load a single image.

name: Dark Web Crawler
blurb: Abstract, censored art generated from scraped (simulated) hidden-service titles.

name: The Quantum Randomizer
blurb: A quantum RNG totally randomizes every CSS class on each load.

name: The Turing Test Chatbot
blurb: The whole site is a chat. Convince the AI you're human to get the URL.

name: The Hallucinating Encyclopedia
blurb: Impossibly detailed fake history — generated citations, fake maps, photos of people who never existed.

name: Subreddit Simulator Live
blurb: A live, fully fake Reddit thread where bots argue with each other in real time.

name: The Sentient Database
blurb: Query a SQL database that replies with snarky, judgmental comments about your JOINs.

name: The Polyglot Server
blurb: The same endpoint, handled by a different backend language on every request.

name: Prompt-Injected E-Commerce
blurb: No Add-to-Cart buttons — you must negotiate or trick the AI chatbot into adding items.

name: The Evolving Dialect
blurb: An AI reinvents the site's language every few seconds. Use context to follow the nav.

name: The Schizophrenic Nav
blurb: The navbar argues with itself; links rename and try to talk you out of clicking them.

name: The Sentient 404
blurb: A 404 that refuses to let you leave, looping links and begging you to stay.

name: Desktop Simulator
blurb: A messy desktop. The About page is buried four folders deep inside taxes_2014.

name: The Endless Staircase
blurb: A parallax illusion of descending an infinite staircase to a footer you'll never reach.

name: The Blackout
blurb: Total dark. Move fast to spark a digital fire bright enough to read the content.

name: The Cult Initiation
blurb: Read the manifesto. Scroll-speed is tracked to be sure you actually read it before unlocking.

name: The Blockchain Labyrinth
blurb: A maze whose walls are re-minted from the hash of each freshly generated block.

name: The Roulette Checkout
blurb: Hit Buy and a wheel spins — black confirms the purchase, red empties your cart and bans you.

name: The Impatient Shopping Cart
blurb: Every 10 seconds you dawdle at checkout, the cart quietly removes one of your items.

name: The Judging AI Cursor
blurb: A second, AI-controlled cursor follows yours and criticizes your erratic mouse control.

name: Procedural GeoCities
blurb: An infinite maze of horrific 1998-style pages — flaming skulls, marquees, visitor counters.

name: The Code Execution Sandbox
blurb: A terminal with root on a disposable Linux that violently resets itself every 10 minutes.

name: The Server Temperature UI
blurb: The background color is tied to the (real) CPU temperature. Stress it and watch it glow red.

name: The 7-Month Mars Ping
blurb: A chat that simulates the speed-of-light delay to Mars — every message takes 14 minutes.

name: The Bribe
blurb: Throttled to 56k dial-up — solve a proof-of-work puzzle to 'pay' for full bandwidth.

name: Password Is the Page
blurb: The password to continue is this entire article. Copy it perfectly into one box.

name: Tab Title Morse Code
blurb: The page is black; the whole article is flashed in Morse code in the browser tab title.

name: The Decoy UI
blurb: The screen is a fake JPEG of a site. The real, interactive site is a 2px speck in the corner.

name: The Base64 URL
blurb: No server at all — the entire HTML/CSS/JS is Base64-encoded directly into the URL.

name: The Password Typo Requirement
blurb: Your password must contain exactly one uppercase, one number, one hieroglyph, and one misspelling.

name: Steganography Gallery
blurb: A normal photo portfolio — but each image hides a ZIP, revealed in the hex inspector.

name: The Architect's Chamber
blurb: The last door. Beyond the experiments lies the one who built them all. Every room you opened is waiting here.
